subject: Ecology
title: Compaction
description: A short lesson to help you apply Compaction.

hook.text: Heavy machinery on farmland crushes soil structure, squeezing out the air roots need.

buildup.text: Compacted soil resists root growth, reduces water infiltration, and kills aerobic microbes.

discovery.text: Once compacted below 30 centimeters, soil may take decades to naturally recover.

twist.text: Livestock overgrazing compacts soil too—hooves on wet pasture destroy structure quickly.

climax.text: Controlled traffic farming confines wheels to permanent lanes, protecting the rest of the field.

punchline.text: Press the soil too hard and it stops working.

quiz.question: Why is soil compaction harmful?
options: It crushes air spaces roots and microbes need
